_comment: The executable form of SPEC §2's tables-extraction algorithm: SQL string literal in, expected `tables` out (sorted; the report emits first-occurrence order, the comparison here is order-insensitive). Every engine MUST extract exactly these sets — two engines extracting different tables from the same SQL would split the AS-EFF-008 policy verdict. run.sh Part 4b embeds each vector in a per-language Db-effect fixture and diffs the reports three-way. Found-in-the-wild divergences get a vector here, like the policy battery.

vectors.name: multiline_join
vectors.sql:
SELECT o.id
FROM orders
JOIN ledger.entries le ON le.oid = o.id

vectors.name: update_leading
vectors.sql: UPDATE users SET name = 'x' WHERE id = 1

vectors.name: for_update_skip
vectors.sql: SELECT id FROM orders FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED

vectors.name: create_if_not
vectors.sql: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS t1 (id INT)

vectors.name: comma_list
vectors.sql: SELECT a FROM t1, t2 JOIN s.t3 ON 1 = 1

vectors.name: with_cte
vectors.sql: WITH x AS (SELECT 1) SELECT * FROM x

vectors.name: insert_select
vectors.sql: INSERT INTO ORDERS SELECT * FROM staging

vectors.name: comma_alias_break
vectors.sql: SELECT a FROM t1 a1, t2 WHERE x = 1
